key_insight: A hybrid UNet-Transformer can translate composite microstructure images directly into detailed stress and strain fields with near-FEM accuracy on most geometries.
engineering_relevance: This enables much faster surrogate evaluation for physics-informed composite design, reducing reliance on expensive finite element simulations.
method: Input: composite geometry images from multiple two-phase tessellations and boundary conditions.
Model: UNet encoder-decoder plus Transformer attention to capture local and long-range mechanical context.
Output: predicts 10 stress/strain field types and uses Grad-CAM/Grad-CAM++ to inspect learned attention.
evidence_signal: The model reports strong performance on most subdatasets, including R2 up to 0.9991 and SSIM up to 0.9936, with MAE below 0.05 on six of eight evaluated subdatasets.
watch_item: Accuracy drops on irregular square-grid cases with sparse soft-phase inclusions, suggesting the model may struggle with sharp stress discontinuities.
paper_title: Hybrid Unet-Transformer Model for Generating Stress and Strain Fields from Composite Geometrics
